name: Date-And-Nut Roll
recipeInstructions:
1. In a heavy, 3-quart saucepan, combine sugar, milk, butter, and corn syrup. Cook over low heat, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon until the sugar dissolves and the mixture comes to a boil; add dates.
2. Continue cooking, stirring occasionally, until a candy thermometer registers 238°F or a little of the mixture forms a soft ball when dropped into cold water.
3. Remove from heat. Add nuts and vanilla extract. Beat until thick. Turn out onto wax paper; divide in half. Shape each half into a roll about 1 3/4 inches in diameter; wrap in waxed paper; refrigerate at least 1 hour before coating with chocolate.
4. In the top of a double boiler, melt chocolate pieces over hot, not boiling, water. Remove from heat.
5. Remove date rolls from refrigerator; spread half of the melted chocolate over each roll, spreading evenly all over. Refrigerate until the chocolate is hard; wrap in waxed paper until ready to cut into slices 1/3 inch thick. Makes 2 1/4 pounds.
